title: Micro-Segment Traffic Baseline Anomaly
dataSources: East-west traffic, segment IDs, flow counts
app: Network flow logs, VMware NSX Add-on, Illumio syslog/HEC, Cisco Secure Workload TA, Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426)
spl:
index=flows sourcetype="netflow"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes, count by src_segment, dest_segment, _time
| eventstats avg(bytes) as avg_bytes by src_segment, dest_segment
| where bytes > (avg_bytes * 5)
| table src_segment, dest_segment, bytes, avg_bytes
description: New or unexpected traffic between segments may indicate lateral movement or misconfiguration. Anomaly detection supports Zero Trust enforcement.
value: New or unexpected traffic between segments may indicate lateral movement or misconfiguration. Anomaly detection supports Zero Trust enforcement.
implementation: Ingest segment-level or flow data. Baseline traffic between segment pairs. Alert when traffic exceeds baseline by threshold. Correlate with new connections and ZT policy. Report on segment traffic matrix.
visualization: Table (anomalous segment pairs), Heatmap (segment √Ч segment traffic), Line chart (traffic trend).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t avg(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern вАФ correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Network flow logs, VMware NSX Add-on, Illumio syslog/HEC, Cisco Secure Workload TA, Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426).
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: East-west traffic, segment IDs, flow counts.
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
Ingest segment-level or flow data. Baseline traffic between segment pairs. Alert when traffic exceeds baseline by threshold. Correlate with new connections and ZT policy. Report on segment traffic matrix.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=flows sourcetype="netflow"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes, count by src_segment, dest_segment, _time
| eventstats avg(bytes) as avg_bytes by src_segment, dest_segment
| where bytes > (avg_bytes * 5)
| table src_segment, dest_segment, bytes, avg_bytes
```

Understanding this SPL

**Micro-Segment Traffic Baseline Anomaly** вАФ New or unexpected traffic between segments may indicate lateral movement or misconfiguration. Anomaly detection supports Zero Trust enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: East-west traffic, segment IDs, flow counts. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Network flow logs, VMware NSX Add-on, Illumio syslog/HEC, Cisco Secure Workload TA, Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: flows; **sourcetype**: netflow. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Scopes the data: index=flows, sourcetype="netflow". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
вАҐ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_segment, dest_segment, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
вАҐ `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_segment, dest_segment** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
вАҐ Filters the current rows with `where bytes > (avg_bytes * 5)` вАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
вАҐ Pipeline stage (see **Micro-Segment Traffic Baseline Anomaly**): table src_segment, dest_segment, bytes, avg_bytes

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Micro-Segment Traffic Baseline Anomaly** вАФ New or unexpected traffic between segments may indicate lateral movement or misconfiguration. Anomaly detection supports Zero Trust enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: East-west traffic, segment IDs, flow counts. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Network flow logs, VMware NSX Add-on, Illumio syslog/HEC, Cisco Secure Workload TA, Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` вАФ enable acceleration for that model.
вАҐ Orders rows with `sort` вАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (anomalous segment pairs), Heatmap (segment √Ч segment traffic), Line chart (traffic trend).
grandmaExplanation: New or unexpected traffic between segments may indicate lateral movement or misconfiguration вАФ so unsafe traffic and policy bypasses are caught quickly.
